description: Registry of clause tags for docs that carry stable clause identifiers and normative language. Tags: 'check-exists' — a named, runnable check already guards the clause (check-ref points at it); 'checkable' — a mechanical check can guard the clause but is not built yet (check-ref names the intended check and any interim probe); 'judgment-only' — compliance is a per-change judgment no check can decide (justification says why). Clause identifiers are stable: never renumbered, and a retired identifier stays reserved (listed under 'retired').

clauses.justification: Whether a novel event source records what the user did or what the code did is a per-change design judgment; no check can classify sources that do not exist yet.

clauses.check-ref: Intended: a static check that recorder/service-worker event registrations stay within the enumerated capture surface (not yet built). Interim probe: the scripted-truth corpus diff (scripts/corpus-compare.js via npm run corpus:check and corpus:check:desktop) reddens when an effect enters a scripted session's stream.

clauses.justification: Whether a piece of observed context describes the actions faithfully and is worth recording alongside them is a design judgment per capture feature.
Whether a new field is context or an action-stream entry in disguise is judgment; no check can decide a field's meaning from its shape.
Applying the definition to a novel input class — is it a physical input the user deliberately performed? — is judgment.
Classifying a novel consequence as effect versus action is judgment; no check can classify consequences that do not exist yet.
Choosing what stands in as the proxy for an out-of-visibility action is a per-proxy design judgment.
Which effect is the immediate one in a new proxy's causal chain is judgment; regressions on existing proxies are probed by the scripted-truth corpus.
Known duplicate pairs are suppressed and unit-tested per instance, but at-most-one-proxy for a newly designed proxy is judgment.
Whether a proxy's recorded shape identifies the user action is judgment.

clauses.check-ref: Scripted-truth corpus redaction sessions ext-redaction and d-redaction (scripts/corpus-compare.js via npm run corpus:check and corpus:check:desktop) verify mask + redacted flag end-to-end on both platforms; the shared detection util packages/shared/lib/field-sensitivity.js is unit-tested; scripts/sufficiency-lint.js enforces the locator masking the contract promises.

clauses.justification: Whether a detection signal is strong enough to trigger masking is a design judgment; no check can decide signal strength.
Applying the signals to a mixed case (the table's Depends rows) or to a signal the table does not list is judgment; no check can decide such a case.

Whether a platform's capture surface is enumerated positively and treated as closed — nothing the platform grows captured implicitly — is a definitional design judgment; the per-platform enumerations are the checkable clauses (ECP-6/DCP-4, probed by the scripted-truth corpus diff).

Whether an interaction belongs on a platform's exception list — the surface description alone would mislead without it — is judgment at review; the same admission test the per-platform ECP-12/DCP-13 apply.

clauses.check-ref: The 200 px both-axes discard is pinned on both platforms: the desktop accumulator and its exactly-200px unit case in packages/desktop/src-tauri/tests/scroll_test.rs and the desktop scroll capture (npm run test:desktop:rust), and the extension shouldCaptureScroll boundary in packages/extension/tests/unit/recorder-logic.test.js (npm run test:extension). The 300 ms coalesce is pinned by the desktop scroll/debounce Rust tests (npm run test:desktop:rust) and, on the extension, by the ext-scroll settle corpus sessions (npm run corpus:check), since the extension coalesce is a live settle-timer no unit test drives.

packages/shared/tests/unit/no-resolution-execution.test.js greps the shipped runtime and build-tooling paths for identifiers unique to the ordered procedure (the corroboration guard, the per-candidate verdict, the containment filter); run via npm run test:shared. The test trees are outside its scan scope — their non-execution is held by the vector machinery’s design (the "resolved" property emerges from counts and equalities, never computed) and by review.

clauses.justification: Whether a new use of recorded match statistics stays a conservative eligibility exclusion or starts deciding outcomes is a per-change judgment about the specification.

No check can observe a consumer implementation’s evaluation order, and an outcome-invariance test would itself execute the procedure, which LR-1 forbids to every Docent artifact; the hygiene locks’ per-candidate re-derivations are order-free by construction, probing only the repo-side surface.
Whether an identity definition for a new platform counts the same distinct elements is judgment; no check can compare automation engines that do not exist yet.

Whether a novel scope or frame construct is uniquely bound is judgment; the no-candidates routing is probed operationally by the sufficiency lint (scripts/sufficiency-lint.js, element-locators predicate).
Whether a schema or specification change keeps entries independent candidates is a semantics judgment no check can decide.
No check can observe a consumer implementation’s evaluation order; the hygiene locks probe the repo-side consequence (every eligible candidate accounted for over committed snapshots).

clauses.check-ref: The hygiene locks (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js) evaluate exactly the eligible set — not masked, match_index not null — over every committed vector snapshot, so the eligibility definition is executable and executed; run via npm run test:shared.

clauses.justification: Whether a new procedure step keeps ineligible entries out of selection and corroboration is judgment about specification edits.

clauses.check-ref: Intended: a check that parses the strategy tables and pins them against the vector-measurement evaluators (not built). Interim probe: lock 5’s query evaluator (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js with packages/shared/tests/unit/vector-measurement.js / packages/shared/tests/unit/vector-measurement-desktop.js; run via npm run test:shared) implements the Application step per strategy over committed snapshots — alignment between the doc tables and the evaluator remains a review judgment.

The coverage-ledger lock (corpus/vectors-coverage.json, locked in packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js) ties every emitted strategy to a committed vector and keeps reserved strategies outside vector scope; run via npm run test:shared.

clauses.justification: Which recorded facts corroborate and which contradict in a novel case is judgment; lock 6 probes the committed vectors’ tag/text agreement with element_facts.
Applying tag equality to a novel control-type mapping is judgment; lock 6 checks committed vectors’ tag agreement exactly.
Whether a text comparison stays a normalized containment test under a specification edit is judgment; lock 6 checks committed vectors’ text containment, including the vacuous-null case.
Whether a field has discriminating power in a novel corroboration case is judgment; no check can decide signal strength.
Whether a specification edit preserves the verdict mapping is judgment; the locks probe only the committed vectors’ counts.
Whether a containment-filter change still operates on recorded facts only, and never collapses beyond what they disambiguate, is judgment.
Whether a new failure mode folds into the three outcomes or mints a fourth is judgment.
Whether diagnostics stay informative rather than decision-bearing is judgment about specification edits.
Whether a proposed vector’s outcome depends on a refinement beyond the procedure is judgment at vector review.
Whether a worked example drifted from the algorithm is judgment at review; the algorithm-governs rule decides the repair direction, not whether drift occurred.

clauses.check-ref: The vector machinery makes the definition executable for the published vectors: lock 5 re-derives every candidate’s matches over the committed snapshot and the produce-stage oracle asserts produced == committed (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js; the corpus vectors run); run via npm run test:shared.

Lock 3 (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js) requires an eligible candidate recorded measured-unique (match_count 1, match_index 0) on every committed vector; run via npm run test:shared.

Every strategy definition on every platform must declare x-value-derived (packages/shared/tests/unit/schema-composition.test.js); scripts/sufficiency-lint.js enforces the masking the annotation promises.

The desktop role_name pinning in packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js exercises the non-localized binding over committed snapshots (localized name fields are placeholder-normalized, so a localized binding could not match); run via npm run test:shared.

packages/shared/tests/unit/no-resolution-execution.test.js guards the shipped surfaces, and the hygiene locks in packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js are structurally counts and committed-field equalities from which the "resolved" property emerges without being computed; run via npm run test:shared.

Intended: a consistency check asserting the authority-ordering statement stays consistent across its echo surfaces (this doc, docs/README.md, README.md, docs/requirements/replay-sufficiency.md, the sync reference README) — not built. Interim: review; the surfaces were hand-aligned in one change, and future edits to them should cite this clause.

Intended: a format prose-pin check comparing the three bump bullets to the classifier rule set (not yet built). Interim probe: the classifier side is pinned by its unit suite (packages/shared/tests/unit/classify-schema-change.test.js; run via npm run test:shared) — prose-to-code alignment stays a review judgment.

The allowlist and escalation live in scripts/classify-schema-change.js (PATCH_ON_ADD_ANNOTATIONS) with unit tests, and the x-value-derived drift guards pin the one known annotation against the real redaction code (packages/shared/tests/unit/schema-composition.test.js and the per-platform guards; run via npm run test:shared). The guard-coupling requirement for a future kind stays a review judgment — no check ties allowlist additions to guard existence.

scripts/check-no-release-outputs.js fails any feature-branch PR that edits schemas/dist/ or bumps a leaf delta version, so hand-bumping cannot merge; the release pipeline computes bumps via scripts/auto-version-schemas.js.

Composition and validator tests derive stamps via stampFromSchema(composePlatform(...)) and pin the const behaviour; every harness relaxation goes through the single clone-only chokepoint relaxVersionStamp (scripts/build-schemas.js), whose harness-locality the backward-compat suite demonstrates (packages/shared/tests/unit/backward-compat.test.js; run via npm run test:shared).
The resolution procedure is implemented by resolveActiveSteps (packages/shared/lib/session.js) and pinned by the shared session unit tests; run via npm run test:shared.

The append-only mutations are pinned by the shared session unit tests, including the tombstone full-copy content and fresh created_at assertions added alongside this clause (packages/shared/tests/unit/session.test.js; run via npm run test:shared).
scripts/sufficiency-lint.js enforces this as the fail-class context-introduced predicate over every linted recording (npm run sufficiency:check).

clauses.justification: Whether a capture change preserves the describe-moment snapshot semantics is judgment; the conformance-vector hygiene locks probe recorded stats against committed snapshots (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js).

clauses.check-ref: Intended: a pin asserting emitted locator order equals the schema strategy declaration order (not built). Interim probe: the scripted-truth corpus truths carry ordered locators, so an emitted-order change diffs against committed truth (npm run corpus:check and corpus:check:desktop).

Intended: a test pinning the unavailable-validator degradation (logged, unvalidated import) and the validate-before-persist ordering (not built). Existing coverage: the bounds, bounds-before-validation order, and validator delegation are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/validate-import.test.js; the stamp const gate by the composed-schema tests (packages/shared/tests/unit/schema-composition.test.js); both run via npm run test:shared.

Intended: a format prose-pin asserting the stated additionalProperties posture over the composed schemas (not yet built). Interim: the schemas are the authority; the composed-schema tests exercise the posture indirectly (npm run test:shared).

The background-layer lint rule (eslint.config.js, no-restricted-syntax ImportExpression) fails any dynamic import at lint time, and packages/extension/tests/unit/service-worker-static-import.test.js guards the entry module; run via npm run lint and npm run test:extension.

Intended: an end-to-end negative asserting the idle surface captures nothing — no recorder injected while no recording runs, and a recorder left in a still-open document stays inactive (not built). Interim probe: the injection e2e specs pin the positive half — injection on record-start and per-frame injection on load (packages/extension/tests/e2e/; npm run test:e2e).

The trust predicate is a pure module pinned by unit tests (packages/extension/lib/frame-trust.js, packages/extension/tests/unit/frame-trust.test.js; run via npm run test:extension), and the frame-trust e2e spec exercises the drop path.

Intended: an append-path unit assertion that a message-body context_id is ignored in favour of the sender-derived one (not built as a dedicated case). Interim probe: the service-worker unit suite exercises append stamping (npm run test:extension); the predicate module never reads a body context_id.

The injection-latency e2e spec measures from the navigation-complete signal (service-worker side) to the readiness timestamp the recorder stamps at ready, same machine wall clock, and enforces the half-floor bound (packages/extension/tests/e2e/specs/injection-latency.spec.js; npm run test:e2e).

Intended: a static check that recorder/service-worker event registrations stay within the enumerated surface (the CP-2 check family; not built). Interim probe: the scripted-truth corpus diff reddens when the surface drifts on scripted sessions (npm run corpus:check).

clauses.justification: Whether a proxy row still records the immediate effect of the chrome interaction it names is a per-proxy judgment (CP-7..CP-10 apply); the corpus ext-tab-open session probes the click-plus-chrome-navigate boundary.

clauses.check-ref: The corpus drives real trusted input only and its diff reddens if synthetic events enter a scripted session (scripts/corpus-compare.js via npm run corpus:check); the recorder unit tests pin the isTrusted rejection (npm run test:extension).

The corpus redaction session verifies mask + text-null + redacted end-to-end (npm run corpus:check); the shared detection util is unit-tested (npm run test:shared); scripts/sufficiency-lint.js enforces the locator masking half.

The url masked-parameter enumeration is pinned to the shared detection set by the schema composition test (packages/shared/tests/unit/schema-composition.test.js; npm run test:shared), and redactUrl is unit-tested (npm run test:shared).

The storage-quota unit tests pin the bands and hysteresis, and two e2e specs pin the pause/override/resume machine including live capture at the boundary and the never-evicts half (quota exhaustion corrupts no stored projects) (packages/extension/tests/e2e/specs/storage-quota.spec.js and storage-quota-live-capture.spec.js; npm run test:extension and test:e2e).

clauses.justification: Whether a proposed entry meets the exceptions admission test hoisted to core CP-15 — the surface description alone would mislead without it — is judgment at review.

Whether a new piece of in-memory service-worker state is a mirror of a persisted key, rebuildable from the browser's own tables, or a safely-losable correlation marker is a design judgment per change; the suspension e2e spec (packages/extension/tests/e2e/specs/service-worker-lifecycle.spec.js) probes the shipped state's survival, not future state.

clauses.check-ref: Intended: a dispatcher-level unit assertion that every panel-protocol request and APPEND_ACTION resolves to exactly one { ok } envelope with failures returned in-band, that GET_TAB_ID answers the bare { tabId } shape and FRAME_READY receives no response, and that APPEND_ACTION acknowledges a trust-gate drop with { ok: true } — delivery, not acceptance — with { ok: false } reserved for storage failures (not built as a dedicated case). Interim probe: the service-worker e2e suite exercises the envelopes across the protocol — success paths, error paths, the unknown-type fallback, and the APPEND_ACTION drop acknowledgment (packages/extension/tests/e2e/specs/service-worker-coverage.spec.js; npm run test:e2e) — and the service-worker unit suite pins individual handlers (packages/extension/tests/unit/service-worker.test.js; npm run test:extension).

Intended: a static scan pinning the extension's chrome.storage write sites (local and session set/remove) to exactly the owners the tables declare (not built). Interim probe: review against the tables; the owning write paths are exercised by the service-worker e2e suites (packages/extension/tests/e2e/specs/service-worker-coverage.spec.js, packages/extension/tests/e2e/specs/service-worker-lifecycle.spec.js; npm run test:e2e) and the adapter unit suite (packages/extension/tests/unit/adapter-chrome.test.js; npm run test:extension).

Intended: a script diffing packages/extension/manifest.json's permissions/host_permissions arrays against the doc's tables (not built). Interim probe: packages/extension/manifest.json resolves to the extension area in the committed area map, whose doc set contains this doc, so every manifest diff primes it for docs disposition.

clauses.justification: Whether a capture change preserves the thread-role split (hooks stay latency-bounded, describes stay on workers) is a design judgment; the input-thread accessibility scope is comment-pinned at its sites.

clauses.check-ref: Ordered insertion, the no-id append-at-arrival (an action without a sequence id passes straight through), and the internal-id strip before store/export are pinned by the desktop reorder-buffer unit tests (packages/desktop/tests/unit/reorder-buffer.test.js; npm run test:desktop); the commit flush barrier's drain-and-continue and sentinel-emitted-last by the worker-pool flush tests (packages/desktop/src-tauri/tests/worker_pool_test.rs regression_298_*; npm run test:desktop:rust), and the bounded fan-out's in-place rescue of a wedged worker by the pool's in-module test flush_all_rescues_a_wedged_worker_in_place_and_still_emits_the_sentinel (packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/capture/worker_pool.rs; same command); and the frontend's sentinel handling — waiting for the sentinel, a sentinel that arrives before the waiter registers, immediate collection with the ids stripped on a no-active-capture (id 0) report, and the rescued-workers warning — by the commitWithCompleteness suite driving the real adapter against a mocked commit_barrier (npm run test:desktop). Named gap: no test drives the frontend wait's timeout (a lost sentinel), so the silent-timeout bound is held by the adapter's bounded-wait comment and review; a timeout pin is the intended closure.
The mode split and mixed recordings are pinned by the capture-mode Rust tests and the corpus coordinate session (npm run test:desktop:rust; npm run corpus:check:desktop).

Intended: a static check that hook/WinEvent registrations stay within the enumerated surface (the CP-2 check family; not built). Interim probe: the desktop corpus diff reddens when the surface drifts on scripted sessions (npm run corpus:check:desktop).

Intended: unit pins for the target-when-set filter, the exclusion-beats-target priority, and the owned-window (system dialog) exclusion including its per-process first-event-wins cache semantics (not built). Existing coverage: the exclusion base rule is pinned by the pid-filter Rust tests, the destroyed-window skip by the scroll module's in-crate tests and the windows module's wrapper test, and the bounded process-tree walk and the executable-name recognition's Docent-binary-name branch by the windows module's in-crate unit tests (the webview-runtime-name branch is a named gap — no test asserts it), with the command normalization of 0/null by the commands tests (npm run test:desktop:rust); the toggle's applied-the-moment-it-changes half is pinned by the desktop self-capture-toggle integration spec (npm run test:integration) — its applied-at-start half is a named gap.

clauses.justification: Whether a proxy row still records the immediate effect of the shell interaction it names is a per-proxy judgment (CP-7..CP-10 apply).

clauses.check-ref: The correlation gates are exercised end-to-end by the enigo integration suites (real input, real WinEvents) and probed by the desktop corpus sessions (npm run test:desktop:rust locally for the integration tier; npm run corpus:check:desktop).
The window-scoping rule is exercised by the capture integration tests (the Save As dialog pre-fill case) via npm run test:desktop:rust.
The buffering supersession is probed by the corpus type-edit session — its known-diff entries track the open supersession defects — via npm run corpus:check:desktop.

Intended: a dedicated classification unit over the press-move-release state machine (not built). Interim probe: the corpus click and double-click sessions pin the click paths (npm run corpus:check:desktop); the drag pair semantics are comment-pinned at the release handler.

The corpus redaction session verifies mask + text-null + redacted end-to-end on desktop (npm run corpus:check:desktop); the password path is pinned by the password Rust tests (npm run test:desktop:rust); scripts/sufficiency-lint.js enforces the locators-pass-untouched half.

The worker-pool invariants (sacred-buffer flush, panic respawn and retry, sticky value-change routing) are pinned by the worker-pool Rust tests (npm run test:desktop:rust). The rescue-drain idempotence is comment-held at the drain sites in packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/capture/worker_pool.rs — a drain-twice pin is intended (not built). The clause's fenced dead-worker limit: the flush fan-out's channel-gone rescue branch and the stop-join/respawn drop-today halves are pinned by no named test — a dead-worker rescue pin is intended, and the drop-today halves are review-held pending the parked drain-at-join/respawn fix.

clauses.justification: Whether a proposed entry meets the exceptions admission test hoisted to core CP-15 — the surface description alone would mislead without it — is judgment at review.

clauses.check-ref: Intended: unit pins for the per-source window attribution and the WinEvent foreground gate (not built). Interim probe: the scripted desktop corpus diff reddens when out-of-scope events enter a scripted session (npm run corpus:check:desktop), and the real-input integration suite drives capture with the gate live (npm run test:desktop:rust).

The reference server integration suite pins store-and-return-verbatim and no-server-side-state (reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/protocol.test.js, reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/round-trip.test.js; run by the server package test script in CI).

The whole-cycle 401/403 halt (before reconcile or push) is pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/auth-failure-halt.property.test.js (npm run test:shared); the server-side token handling by reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/auth.test.js.

Manifest shape and fields are pinned by reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/protocol.test.js; the client-side connection test that reuses this endpoint by packages/shared/tests/unit/connection-test.test.js (npm run test:shared).

PUT semantics (path/body id match, verbatim replacement, 200/201) are pinned by reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/protocol.test.js and reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/round-trip.test.js.

Intended: a unit pinning that unrecognized top-level payload fields are ignored on pull (not built as a dedicated case). Interim: the pull path reconstructs projects through an allowlist, so unknown fields are dropped by construction (packages/shared/sync-client.js).
The pull-before-push ordering and its divergence-detection consequence are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/pull-precedes-push.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The capture halt by packages/shared/tests/unit/capture-active-halt.property.test.js, the pending-actions halt by packages/shared/tests/unit/pending-action-protection.property.test.js, and the inbound locked exclusion by packages/shared/tests/unit/locked-recording-exclusion.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The stamp gate (skip-and-report, never a conflict, per-project) is pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/stamp-compatibility.test.js; pulled-payload schema validation (invalid is an error, never a conflict, per-project) by packages/shared/tests/unit/safeguard-composition.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The digest-based classification is pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/classify-decision-table.property.test.js and packages/shared/tests/unit/classify-deterministic.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).
Baseline advancement (agreement/adoption only, never push) is pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/baseline-advancement.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The two conflict-producing situations (and only those) are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/divergence-conflict.property.test.js, packages/shared/tests/unit/delete-vs-change-conflict.property.test.js, and packages/shared/tests/unit/no-baseline-no-one-sided-change.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The identity-based append-only-and-nothing-else predicate is pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/fast-forward-superset.property.test.js; the deletions toggle’s auto-apply outcomes by packages/shared/tests/unit/reported-counts.property.test.js; recording-level scoping (project metadata never a fast-forward candidate) by packages/shared/tests/unit/interaction-free-automatic.property.test.js (npm run test:shared). The declined-version-is-never-auto-applied cross-reference shares SP-18's named gap.

Per-recording assembly, the no-omission rule, deferred/locked version selection, and the content-decided skip are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/per-unit-push-assembly.property.test.js, packages/shared/tests/unit/locked-recording-push-preservation.property.test.js, and packages/shared/tests/unit/changed-local-outgoing-push.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The stored-project If-Match rows (match proceeds with fresh ETag; stale 412 leaves the store unchanged) are pinned by reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/conditional-write.test.js; the no-project-stored 412 branch is pinned at the decision-function level by reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/unit/conditional-write.test.js (an end-to-end case for that branch is a named gap).

The pull GETs' cache bypass is pinned by the dedicated "issues every GET with cache: 'no-store'" case in packages/shared/tests/unit/sync-client.test.js (npm run test:shared); the connection test sets the same bypass at its single request site (packages/shared/connection-test.js) — a dedicated pin for that request is a named gap.

Retain-every-record and tombstones-stay-tombstoned are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/resolution-append-only-tombstone.property.test.js; the no-default rejection of an absent choice by packages/shared/tests/unit/resolution-clear-vs-retain.property.test.js (with the explicit DELETE_RESOLUTION path driven by packages/shared/tests/unit/resolve-conflict-fixtures.test.js); post-resolution recoverability by packages/shared/tests/unit/resolution-recoverability.property.test.js (npm run test:shared). Named gap: those suites drive resolveConflict with hand-built resolved states — no check pins the shipped construction paths themselves to chosen-side-active-view-wins; a builder-level pin covering both platforms' construction is the intended closure.

Intended: a two-cycle unit case pinning that a unit already held for review is auto-applied on the first cycle after Auto-accept-updates is enabled, and a payload pin that no settings field enters a pushed body (not built). Interim probe: settings are read from the store at each cycle's reconcile (getSettings in packages/shared/sync-client.js applyAutomaticOutcomes), the per-cycle policy application is pinned across all four settings combinations by packages/shared/tests/unit/policy-settings-scope.property.test.js, and the assembled push payload carries only docent_format/project/recordings (packages/shared/tests/unit/per-unit-push-assembly.property.test.js; npm run test:shared).

Keep-local/never-push, the exact-version dismissal digest, the deletion-marker dismissal, and retained recoverability are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/decline-review.property.test.js; the reconcile phase honoring a recorded dismissal on later cycles (same version suppressed, different version classified afresh) by packages/shared/tests/unit/resolution-propagates-next-cycle.property.test.js (npm run test:shared). Named gap: no check combines a recorded dismissal with Auto-accept updates enabled, so the dismissal-precedes-auto-apply guard order in packages/shared/sync-client.js is unpinned.

The atomic persist-or-nothing block and the internal-error halt (store never written, local projects returned unchanged, nothing pushed) are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/internal-failure-blocking.property.test.js and packages/shared/tests/unit/abort-on-unretainable.property.test.js (npm run test:shared). The shipped client message is presentation and stays review-held.

Both deliveries — the pull-phase halt before the store is touched and the push-phase halt after the persisted reconcile — are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/auth-failure-halt.property.test.js (failure points: pull-manifest, pull-project, push); the no-PUT-after-a-pre-push-halt ordering by packages/shared/tests/unit/pull-precedes-push.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

Intended: unit pins that the extension's AUTO_SYNC_DATA_EVENTS set (packages/extension/background/service-worker.js) and the desktop panel's notifyDataEvent call sites cover exactly the six events, and that both backstops use BACKSTOP_INTERVAL_MS (not built). Interim probe: backstop-triggered background cycles are exercised end-to-end by packages/extension/tests/unit/auto-sync-host.test.js (chrome.alarms) and packages/desktop/tests/unit/auto-sync-host.test.js (interval + data-event hooks) (npm run test:extension / npm run test:desktop).

Coalescing (at most one cycle per cooldown window, a single pending follow-up), the never-overlap guarantee, the capture-active drop-not-queue, and failure-survival are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/sync-scheduler.property.test.js and packages/shared/tests/unit/sync-scheduler.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The fingerprint-exact enable rule, settings-change invalidation, and the auth auto-disable transitions are pinned against the real shared helpers (connection-test fingerprint, sync-store settings, scheduler stop) by packages/shared/tests/unit/sync-settings-state-machine.test.js; the desktop background host's auth-disable policy by packages/desktop/tests/unit/auto-sync-host.test.js (npm run test:shared / npm run test:desktop). The panel DOM wiring is mirrored, not imported, in the state-machine test — that residue is review-held.

clauses.justification: Whether a truth edit derives from the script and the capture principles rather than enshrining recorder output is judgment at the mandatory line-by-line review.

Whether a missing fact is format-expressible (corpus territory) or not (the sufficiency lint’s gap territory) is a per-fact judgment; truth files’ schema validity is enforced by their membership in the lint corpus (npm run sufficiency:check).

clauses.check-ref: The both-direction baseline lock is implemented by scripts/corpus-compare.js and pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/corpus-compare.test.js (npm run test:shared); it gates CI via npm run corpus:check and corpus:check:desktop.

Intended: a page lint rejecting external resources and time/locale-dependent constructs in committed session pages (not built). Interim: corpus/serve.js pins the fixed loopback port, and pages are reviewed against the authoring rules.

The closed kinds, strategy cross-check, masked-entry refusal, the 0-exact scroll class map, and the every-entry-applies validation are implemented by scripts/corpus-compare.js and pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/corpus-compare.test.js (npm run test:shared); the alignment-scoping behaviour and the CLI exit-code mapping stay review-held.

clauses.justification: Whether a new page obeys the determinism rules is review judgment; cross-run stability of the corpus in CI is the operational probe.
Whether a driver API produces trusted input on a novel interaction is judgment; a violation surfaces as a missing action in the session’s own diff.

clauses.check-ref: The pipeline runs end-to-end on producer-generated dumps: the Rust corpus test produces them (locally on Windows; the Windows CI job uploads them as an artifact), then npm run corpus:assemble:desktop replays them through the real adapter and npm run corpus:check:desktop diffs against committed truth.

clauses.justification: Whether a new session’s design stays environment-independent (no programmatic raise, click-driven activation) is judgment; CI retry-budget stability is the operational probe.

clauses.check-ref: The produce-stage oracle asserts produced == committed at the two production sites (packages/extension/tests/e2e/corpus/corpus.spec.js exact; scripts/corpus-assemble-desktop-vectors.js under the comparator class rules), and the hygiene locks re-derive the committed vectors’ counts (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js; npm run test:shared).

The locks are counts and equalities by construction, and packages/shared/tests/unit/no-resolution-execution.test.js guards that no shipped surface implements the ordered procedure (npm run test:shared).

The ledger lock ties every emitted strategy to a committed vector or a reasoned gap (corpus/vectors-coverage.json, locked in packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js; npm run test:shared).

clauses.justification: The coverage boundary is a scope statement; whether a change moves behaviour across it (into the unexercised bridge, UI, or persistence surfaces) is judgment.
Whether a retirement reason is honest and a removal decision is the maintainer’s are process judgments no check can decide.

clauses.check-ref: The desktop hygiene locks run over the committed placeholder-normalized snapshots, so a snapshot freezing an OS-locale Name diverges from the locks’ re-derivations (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js; npm run test:shared); the normalization itself is applied by scripts/corpus-assemble-desktop-vectors.js.

clauses.justification: Whether a vector’s element facts were captured through the real desktop path rather than hand-authored is provenance judgment at review; lock 2 checks internal consistency, not origin.

clauses.check-ref: Lock 2 permits exactly the labeled_by/tree_path additive-stats augmentation and nothing else, and lock 5 re-derives the harness-measured pairs over the committed snapshot (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js; npm run test:shared).

clauses.justification: Whether a produced-vs-committed divergence is an environment variance the comparator class rules absorb or a deliberate re-baseline moment (a runner-image bump) is judgment; the produce-stage oracle reddens either way.

Whether a specification edit keeps the Request/Payload/Response sections a sufficient endpoint contract — the client-binding sections acquiring no endpoint obligations beyond the named DI-15 exception — is an editorial judgment no check can decide.

clauses.check-ref: The Bearer header presence/omission is pinned by the 'includes Authorization header when apiKey provided' and 'omits Authorization header when apiKey is null' cases in packages/shared/tests/unit/dispatch-core.test.js (npm run test:shared) and by the API-key describe in packages/extension/tests/unit/dispatch.test.js (npm run test:extension). Named gap: no test pins the POST method or the Content-Type header; a request-shape pin is the intended closure.

The exact five-field wrapper is asserted by packages/shared/tests/unit/contract.test.js ('has exactly five top-level keys') and packages/extension/tests/unit/dispatch.test.js (npm run test:shared / test:extension); the three-field export contrast is pinned by the desktop export suite validating buildExport output against the closed schema root (packages/desktop/tests/unit/export.test.js; npm run test:desktop); the stamp-or-fail sourcing by the stampFromSchema throw path (packages/shared/lib/format-stamp.js, exercised by the composition suites; npm run test:shared).

Field-for-field payload fidelity is pinned by 'buildPayload preserves all project, recording, and step fields' in packages/extension/tests/unit/dispatch.test.js (npm run test:extension) and the metadata/step-mode projections by packages/shared/tests/unit/dispatch-payload.test.js; tombstone retention by 'preserves deleted flag on steps' in packages/shared/tests/unit/dispatch-core.test.js (npm run test:shared). That pending actions live outside recording.steps is the shared session model's structure, review-held here.

Intended: a pin requiring the shipped guidance asset to be re-confirmed whenever the schema source layers or this wrapper specification change (not built). Interim: review — whether a semantics or wrapper change invalidates the guidance prose is judged per change.

2xx-with-unparseable-body success ('returns empty object when response is not valid JSON'), per-status DispatchError surfacing (400/401/403/404/500/502), and adversarial response tolerance are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/dispatch-core.test.js and packages/shared/tests/unit/security.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The retry/backoff describe in packages/shared/tests/unit/dispatch-core.test.js pins 503 and 429 retries, the maxRetries exhaustion path, the fail-fast on non-transient 4xx, Retry-After honouring via injected sleep, and network-error retries (npm run test:shared); the AbortError case in the same file pins timeout classification (transient) only — the 30-second per-attempt duration is comment-pinned at its call site and review-held.

clauses.justification: The delivery posture is the design consequence of DI-7's retry mechanics — no repository check can observe an endpoint's duplicate receipt. The mechanically checkable byte-identity half is split out as DI-16.

clauses.check-ref: Both bounds are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/dispatch-core.test.js: 'throws DispatchError with null status for payload > 50MB' (before any request) and 'throws DispatchError for response > 10MB' (declared Content-Length); npm run test:shared.

The rule set is pinned by the validateEndpointUrl describes in packages/shared/tests/unit/dispatch-core.test.js (scheme, malformed, embedded credentials, empty-clears, link-local block, the plaintext-HTTP-with-key guard and its loopback allowance) and the embedded-credential/scheme cases in packages/shared/tests/unit/security.test.js (npm run test:shared); the save-time wiring by the invalid-URL settings cases in packages/desktop/tests/integration/panel-dispatch-sync.spec.js (npm run test:integration) — a dedicated pin for the extension panel's save-time wiring is a named gap (the extension adapter re-validates in saveSettings, exercised by packages/extension/tests/unit/dispatch.test.js).

The native URL policy (https to any host, http only to loopback, link-local rejected on either scheme, non-HTTP schemes and hostless URLs rejected) is pinned by the validate_sync_url unit tests in packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/sync_http.rs (npm run test:desktop:rust); the 30 s timeout constant and the single-chokepoint claim are comment-pinned at the command and review-held.

The seam contract — lazy fetch default, bound-transport routing, reset, non-function fallback — is pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/http-transport.test.js (npm run test:shared); the desktop binding is exercised end-to-end by the integration suites driving the real adapter against a mocked sync_http_request command (packages/desktop/tests/integration/panel-dispatch-sync.spec.js; npm run test:integration).

'dispatch button disabled without endpoint configured' and 'dispatch flow shows confirmation with step count' in packages/desktop/tests/integration/panel-dispatch-sync.spec.js pin the endpoint gate and the confirmation surface (npm run test:integration). Named gaps: the active-steps eligibility filter and the extension panel's mirrored gating have no dedicated pins and are review-held.

The cooldown tracker — block after send, countdown, boundary-as-elapsed, clock-skew tolerance, and the 5 s default — is pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/dispatch-cooldown.test.js (npm run test:shared); the panels' DOM wiring (disable, hint, no cooldown on failure) is review-held.

clauses.justification: A deployment-time configuration on an operator's endpoint; no repository check can observe what CORS headers a deployed endpoint emits.

clauses.check-ref: Intended: a unit case pinning single-serialization — byte-identical bodies across retry attempts (not built). Interim probe: the serialization call site precedes the attempt loop in packages/shared/dispatch-core.js.

clauses.justification: Whether a piece of logic is behaviour-defining (shared-layer territory) or platform wiring (adapter/panel territory) is a per-change design judgment, and no static check can recognize a platform-local reimplementation of semantics the shared layer already defines.

clauses.check-ref: The 'Verify sync-shared output is fresh' CI step (.github/workflows/test.yml) re-runs npm run sync-shared on the PR tree and fails on any git diff in the committed assembled index.html files, so a stale or hand-edited committed copy cannot merge. The gitignored synced trees are outside that diff's reach: for them the rule is held by regeneration (every sync deletes and re-copies the tree) and review. The clause's release-pipeline half (each publish workflow syncs before packaging) is likewise held by the workflow text and review, not by the named freshness check.

Intended: a script diffing the crate's #[tauri::command] functions and the generate_handler! registration in packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs against this doc's table, and the grants in packages/desktop/src-tauri/capabilities/default.json against the doc's stated plugin surface (not built). Interim probe: packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs and capabilities/default.json both resolve to the desktop-windows area in the committed area map, whose doc set contains this doc, so command-registration and grant diffs alike prime it for docs disposition; the single capture:action emit site is review-held (the emit call in lib.rs names the channel; nothing pins uniqueness mechanically).

The strip/re-inject pair is pinned by the in-module unit tests in packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/secret_store.rs (strip_then_inject_round_trips asserts the on-disk text contains neither key; strip_deletes_store_entry_for_empty_string and strip_leaves_store_untouched_when_field_absent pin the clear-vs-omit semantics) and at the command wrappers by the secret_wrappers tests in packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands.rs (strip_removes_api_key_from_written_json, inject_restores_api_key_into_read_json); run via npm run test:desktop:rust. Named residue: that no other code path writes the session file is held by the file I/O living only in commands.rs (session_file_path is module-private) and review, not by a named check.

Intended: a red-path unit pin feeding a schema-invalid document and an unknown platform stamp to lintFile and asserting the refusal (not built). Interim probe: the corpus baseline lock (packages/shared/tests/unit/sufficiency-lint.test.js, npm run test:shared) routes every corpus member through the same validating pass — lintFile validates against the composed schema and throws before any predicate runs (scripts/sufficiency-lint.js).

Findings are serialized class-first ("<class>:<id> <pointer>") by the lint's toBaseline, so the corpus baseline lock reddens on reclassification of any predicate with baseline entries, and the per-predicate pins hold the rest on minimal documents; the union covers all predicates, neither mechanism alone is universal (packages/shared/tests/unit/sufficiency-lint.test.js; npm run test:shared).

assertGapPredicatesCurrent (scripts/sufficiency-lint.js) probes the live composed schema on every lint pass and throws on an obsolete gap predicate, so the corpus baseline lock (packages/shared/tests/unit/sufficiency-lint.test.js, npm run test:shared), npm run sufficiency:check, and the comparator's --lint runs all refuse to pass the moment a composed schema grows a probed capability. Named residue: that every gap predicate carries a probe at all is review-held — the assertion skips a probe-less gap predicate; the one-line throw that would close it is a known candidate hardening.

valueDerivedStrategies (scripts/sufficiency-lint.js) reads x-value-derived from the composed contract and throws on an undeclared, const-less, or value-less strategy def; the unit suite pins the derived set per platform against the annotation (packages/shared/tests/unit/sufficiency-lint.test.js), every strategy def's explicit declaration is enforced by packages/shared/tests/unit/schema-composition.test.js, and the per-platform drift guards pin the annotation against the real redaction code (packages/extension/tests/unit/service-worker.test.js, packages/desktop/tests/unit/adapter-tauri.test.js).

The both-direction lock is packages/shared/tests/unit/sufficiency-lint.test.js (npm run test:shared), with file discovery and baseline serialization imported from scripts/sufficiency-lint.js so the walk, filters, and entry format cannot diverge from --write-baseline output (the two-root list is stated separately in lock and npm script; a divergence there fails the lock loudly); npm run sufficiency:check is the same gate at the CLI. The lock reddens on any predicate or corpus change that shifts findings, forcing the regenerated baseline into the same change; whether a regenerated baseline was honestly reviewed stays review judgment.
